name: Data Visualization
description: A visual representation technique used to transform structured or unstructured data into perceptually interpretable forms, enabling efficient exploration of patterns, trends, and the space of possible affirmations about the underlying reality.

name: Standard Deviation
description: A statistical measure of dispersion that quantifies the extent of variability or spread in a dataset relative to its mean, enabling affirmations about consistency, volatility, and distributional structure.

name: Dimensionality Reduction
description: A technique that identifies the directions (principal components) along which the variation in the data is maximized, facilitating recognition of latent structures and core dynamics in complex datasets.

name: Regression Analysis
description: A method to model and quantify the relationship between variables, allowing affirmations about causality, predictive trends, and dependency structures in data.

name: Clustering
description: A technique to partition data into groups based on similarity, supporting the identification of emergent categories, coherent patterns, and structural organization in complex reality.

name: Network Analysis
description: A framework for mapping and quantifying relationships and interactions between entities, enabling affirmations about connectivity, influence, and systemic structure.

name: Bayesian Inference
description: A probabilistic reasoning method that updates beliefs about reality in light of evidence, facilitating affirmations under uncertainty and modeling conditional dependencies.

name: Time Series Analysis
description: A set of methods to analyze temporal data, detect trends, seasonality, and anomalies, and generate affirmations about dynamic processes evolving over time.

name: Monte Carlo Simulation
description: A computational method to estimate the behavior of complex systems under uncertainty by random sampling, enabling affirmations about distributions, risks, and expected outcomes.

name: Hypothesis Testing
description: A statistical procedure to assess assumptions about reality, facilitating affirmations about the presence of effects, differences, or associations in data.

name: Burstiness Index
description: A statistical measure that quantifies the degree of temporal clustering in a sequence of events. It facilitates reasoning about variability and regularity in event arrivals, supporting affirmations about patterns, dependencies, and deviations from expected randomness.
